sections.h:
The main assumptions made in this part of the manual are:

• You are familiar with the Windows Operating System

• You are familiar with the browser being used to access ACOS.

• ACOS operates best on Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge browsers.

(It is recommended not to use Microsoft Internet Explorer in the WAPOL environment.) Any browser messages that appear whilst using ACOS will be displayed in the style appropriate to the browser being used.
sections.b: The main assumptions made in this part of the manual are: • You are familiar with the Windows Operating System • You are familiar with the browser being used to access ACOS. • ACOS operates best on Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge browsers. (It is recommended not to use Microsoft Internet Explorer in the WAPOL environment.) Any browser messages that appear whilst using ACOS will be displayed in the style appropriate to the browser being used.

sections.h:
The timeout delay period is 60 minutes. This means that after 60 minutes of no browser refresh activity, mouse clicking, typing or tabbing between fields, you will be automatically logged out of the system and no data will be saved. There will be a warning, which pops up 3 minutes before you will be logged out. Clicking on the pop up or on any other part of the screen will cancel the warning and allow you to stay in ACOS and continue working.

ACOS Vs PEGASUS – NAME CHANGE IN 2023

Altia ACOS is the new name for ABM Pegasus, Altia Pegasus, or Pegasus.

Altia purchased ABM and renames Pegasus as ACOS (Altia Covert Operation System) due to a conflicting international software name. WAPOL use multiple ACOS modules.

This one is called the ACOS Source Management module, but we call it “ACOS”

sections.t: Finding & Replacing Text in Text Editor
sections.h:
In the Text Editor, users can quickly search for every occurrence of a specific word or phrase, and replace it with another word or phrase. For example, they can replace the word “simular” with “similar”.

To find and replace all matching items in the

Text Editor, select the Global radio button.

The search facility is case sensitive, so

users may need to select the Global, case

insensitive radio button if the case of the entered text does not exactly match the case of the text the user is searching for.

Click on the Find/Replace button.

sections.h:
A drop-down list is a pre-defined list of options from which users need to select an appropriate entry for the corresponding data field. Users will be able to recognise such lists by the down arrow button positioned on the right-hand side of the data field.

To use the drop-down list, click on the down arrow button in the required box.
sections.b: A drop-down list is a pre-defined list of options from which users need to select an appropriate entry for the corresponding data field. Users will be able to recognise such lists by the down arrow button positioned on the right-hand side of the data field. To use the drop-down list, click on the down arrow button in the required box.

sections.h:
A List of Values (LOV) is a predefined list of options, which allows you to select an appropriate entry for the corresponding data field instead of manually typing it. The list always contains an initial blank entry, so that the value can be cleared or left blank if appropriate.

You will recognise the LOV buttons as they are positioned on the right-hand side of the field.

Click on the LOV button in the required box. For shorter lists, double click the desired value in the list to select it.

For longer lists, you can enter the word or some of the letters of the word before or after the wild card (%) in the Find field and click on the Find button.

This will filter the list so only the entries that finish with the specified letters are shown.

sections.t: Notifications - Sending Documents to Another User
sections.h:
You may need to send (Notify) documents to other users for approval/authorisation; or to forward reviewed ‘paperwork’ back to other users.

You can do so by:

• Clicking on the Notify iconic button - in this case the document will be sent as an

attachment to any recipients pre-defined by the system. You are able to add extra recipients if required. When sending by this method the Process State of the document will change.

• Clicking on the Forward button - in this case the document will also be sent as an

attachment, but no pre-defined recipients will exist, so you will need to search and select them. There will be NO change in the Process State when sending by this method.

To send a document to another user via the Notify button:

Open the relevant document and click on the Notify button. At this stage a validation check will be performed and if there are any incomplete mandatory fields you will be prevented from proceeding.

This is covered later.

The recipients will be auto-populated if a notification path has been configured.

The Subject and Main Notification Text will auto-populate based on the specific notification event appropriate to the record you are in and the process state.

Optional: Record a message in the User Notification Text box.

Optional: Under Return If Unread In enter a number of hours after which, if the recipient hasn’t read the message, you will receive a notification stating this.

• Select time critical

Click on the Send button. The notification will be sent, together with the appropriate document attached to it (e.g., Contact Sheet).

Note: The default recipient of a message is determined by the system. To add more recipients, click on the add button.

An Outlook advice email is sent when any notification or message is generated in ACOS.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. sections.h:
The workflow in ACOS depends on users responding to notifications in a timely manner. Some users get confused about what they have done and what they must do.

The solution is to finish every action by returning to the inbox, and deleting the notification you just dealt with.

WARNINGS

A warning is a message recorded against a main entity record, which is used to inform you of important information about a specific record.

Manual warnings can be created by any authorised user, while automatic warnings are produced by ACOS when certain conditions are met. The example shows a warning that a person is on bail which is set via the nominal court appearances record.
sections.b: The workflow in ACOS depends on users responding to notifications in a timely manner. Some users get confused about what they have done and what they must do. The solution is to finish every action by returning to the inbox, and deleting the notification you just dealt with. WARNINGS A warning is a message recorded against a main entity record, which is used to inform you of important information about a specific record. Manual warnings can be created by any authorised user, while automatic warnings are produced by ACOS when certain conditions are met. The example shows a warning that a person is on bail which is set via the nominal court appearances record.

sections.h:
Under certain circumstances, warnings will be automatically assigned to the nominal entity based upon the following conditions:

- Juvenile Person - is of juvenile age;

- Possible Juvenile Person - when an age is estimated;

- Person in Prison - has effective Prison Details entered;

- Person on Bail - has a Court Appearance record with a Bail outcome;
sections.b: Under certain circumstances, warnings will be automatically assigned to the nominal entity based upon the following conditions: - Juvenile Person - is of juvenile age; - Possible Juvenile Person - when an age is estimated; - Person in Prison - has effective Prison Details entered; - Person on Bail - has a Court Appearance record with a Bail outcome;

sections.h: In Altia ACOS when users open a document that is linked to an entity record with a warning, users will see the warning text shown in the Information Bar in the header area.
sections.b: In Altia ACOS when users open a document that is linked to an entity record with a warning, users will see the warning text shown in the Information Bar in the header area.

sections.h:
The text colour of the warning indicates its priority level. The system currently uses five priority levels (can be increased to 12).

Three general warnings are High (RED), Medium ((YYEELLLLOOWW)),, Low (GREEN).

Two task related warnings are Renewal (ORANGE), and Review (BLUE).

sections.h:
Locate the relevant Nominal record within a document and click on Details.

Once the Nominal Details screen opens click on the Warnings button on the top menu bar.

This opens the Warnings List showing existing warnings linked to the record.

Click on the ‘New’ icon on the iconic toolbar.

Click the LOV button next to the Warning Type box, and select the relevant warning definition (e.g. Dangerous) from the drop-down List of Values (LOV).

Optional: Use the Additional

Information box to record any extra information related to this warning.

Click Save
sections.b: Locate the relevant Nominal record within a document and click on Details. Once the Nominal Details screen opens click on the Warnings button on the top menu bar. This opens the Warnings List showing existing warnings linked to the record. Click on the ‘New’ icon on the iconic toolbar. Click the LOV button next to the Warning Type box, and select the relevant warning definition (e.g. Dangerous) from the drop-down List of Values (LOV). Optional: Use the Additional Information box to record any extra information related to this warning. Click Save

sections.h:
In some instances, a warning may require amending, extending or reduce the active period of the warning. The warning record may also require update when the warning is due to expire.

The ability to update a warning will normally be restricted to the officer who originally added the warning, and those users set as recipients of the notification on expiry (if appropriate).

Once the warning record has been reviewed, it can be modified as needed, or allowed to expire as defined if no other evidence exists to justify the retention of the warning.

The following updates can be made:

• Effective Start Date – if this date is set in the future, you can bring it forward to activate the

warning. You cannot modify the Start Date for active or expired warnings.

• Effective End Date – can be lengthened or reduced as required.

• You can remove a warning by setting the ‘Effective End Date’ to the current date and setting the

Action on Expiry to Remove. Once the save/select button has been pressed and the profile saved, the warning will be expired and removed.

sections.h:
The use of an ARD should not be disclosed to the Source and the audio recording must not be saved to any other device or hard drive unless using OneDrive for the purposes of uploading content of a OneForce phone. ARDs utilised must have the ability to be pin locked. ARDs should only have internal storage and no removable SD Card. ARDs that have the ability to digitally encrypt audio are preferred, however, are not mandated for use.

The following procedures apply to the use of ARDs and recordings:

• All contact with a Source must be audio recorded.

• Any contact involving payment to a Source (regardless of Tier) must be audio recorded.

Handlers are responsible for ensuring ARDs are ready for use, and where operationally possible used for the recording of all meetings and payments.

• The Supervisor is to ensure audio recordings are uploaded to ACOS in a timely manner.

• Audio file names should conform with the naming convention as provided by the CR and

cross referenced with the relevant Contact Sheet;

• Handlers must delete audio recordings from the ARD once upload has been completed and

checked to ensure it is playable.

• All audio recordings must comply with Section 5(3) of the Surveillance Devices Act 1989.

• If for some reason the contact isn’t or cannot be recorded, the contact description is to fully

account for the conversation had with the Source and the reason the conversation was not audio recorded.

• There is no requirement to audio record meetings with a Tier 1 (Community Source) unless

making a payment to that person.

Handlers and Supervisors must ensure disclosure obligations are complied with. Where a legislative requirement dictates, the Supervisor should contact the Investigating Officer and ensure that a Non- Disclosure Order is considered/obtained and an appropriate record made on the Sensitive Material List.

The naming conventions for contacts

uploaded on ACOS are as follows;

Audio Recording Devices (ARD) Application

The following ARD and methods of use are approved for recording contacts with Sources.

Use of One Force Phone to record from a second phone that is on loud speaker. This is to comply with legislation.

• Upload recording to One Drive on your One Force Phone.

• Upload recording to your ACOS Contact. Check it has been uploaded and plays.

• Delete recording off your One Force Phone immediately as per the HSMM.

• Delete recording from OneDrive immediately or;

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 21 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 Use an Olympus 9000 recorder (or similar) and record using a TP7 or TP8 (earpiece with microphone capability) linked to the internal storage on the recorder. (The Olympus 9000 is an example of a Pin Locked internal storage only device that is a preferred model for use).

Ensure the device is pin locked with the universal pin number supplied by HSMU.

Plug device into computer.

Convert to MP3 and upload to ACOS contact. Check it has been uploaded and plays.

Delete the recording from the recorder.

ATTACHMENTS

All users can attach any type of electronic file to the system.

ACOS allows for upload of files over 100mb, but it is recommended to keep the files as small as possible. (100mb takes over 5 minutes to upload). When creating/recording a file, consider segmenting larger files into separate parts. (Eg- a 3-hour audio file of 300mb will need to be 3 parts) You can add various Attachments to documents, such as scanned photographs, electronic versions of documents, digital CCTV recordings etc.

Click then ‘New Attachment’, button on the iconic toolbar (the document can be in either View or Edit mode).

You will be taken to the appropriate attachment summary screen showing all existing attachments linked to this document.

The Document Summary column under the Other Attachments section of the attachment summary screen provides more explicit details of the documents that other attachments have been made against.

A folder structure is available to allow you to manage your attachments linked to a document or entity.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 22 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 Clicking the New Upload icon on the iconic toolbar will allow you to create a new attachment.

Enter a description of the file being uploaded in to the ‘Original File Information’ field.

Check that the Document Context field contains the correct value.

Click on the Browse button in the ‘File to Upload’ box and locate the file that you wish to load into the ACOS database.

Clicking Browse will allow you to locate the file you wish to load into the database. Use the free text box to record information about the file.

sections.h:
When a title is copied from another Widows app like Word or Outlook, a short dash (-) defaults to a long dash (–) and the attachment will fail.

IT is hard to notice the difference, so it is best to never use dashes in the title.

(E.G., ‘TEST - memo - 1’ will work and ‘TEST - memo – 1’ will fail.) Click Upload button on the iconic toolbar.

You are then prompted to select a folder into, which to upload the attachment.

Select the required folder and click on OK.

This will link the attachment to the record. You will then be returned to the Attachments Summary screen, which defaults to the Root Document folder. You will need to select the folder into, which you have just uploaded the attachment to view it.

If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 23 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 If there are several files to upload, before you click

on the UPLOAD button, you may select the Add

Record icon in the file upload block and select

the next file in the same way as the first. The

original file information text will be copied but you

may edit or delete it and add more.

When all files are selected, click the UPLOAD icon

and the file(s) will be uploaded to the selected

folder and stored in the database as an

attachment against the selected documents,

copies of the file will be stored separately against each document.

sections.h:
When a Source is tasked as a civilian participant it is considered high risk. The Source is given authority to engage in particular ‘controlled conduct’, at the direction of a Handler who must be Level 3 Human Source Management Trained, and hold NV 1 accreditation.

Process requirements for a source to be authorised as a Civilian Participant must meet the requirements of the Criminal Investigation (Covert Powers) Act 2012.

• The Principal Law Enforcement Officer (PLEO) for the Controlled Operation is responsible for

submitting an email to the Central Registrar See below example

• The Central Registrar then provides the Inspector, Crime Support & Services Division with

recommendations about the proposed use of the source as a civilian participant.

• The Inspector, Crime Support & Services Division considers suitability of the Source to

engage as a civilian participant and rejects or authorises it.

• The ‘Handler’ is required to commence an AUTHORISATION for PARTICIPATION –

(HANDLER) which includes additional risks and control measures particular to the source acting as a civilian participant.

EXAMPLE – REQUEST TO TASK SOURCE AS A CIVILLIAN PARTICIPANT

sections.t: Handler - How to Create A New Registration
sections.h: The registration process consists of three documents, the Source Profile, Authorisation and the Risk Assessment.
sections.b: The registration process consists of three documents, the Source Profile, Authorisation and the Risk Assessment.

sections.h:
1. From the Home Screen select Menu / Source Management / Register

You will now see a Source Profile Screen.

Opening a new Source Profile screen automatically creates a new Source URN (Unique Reference Number also referred to as SURN).

WARNING – Every time you click ‘Register’ when not required, you will generate a new Source Profile with a Source URN that cannot be deleted.

The Source Profile process state is

2. Complete CURRENT EVALUATION. – Often begins as ‘F – Reliability cannot be judged’ and

should be updated regularly as this rating is automatically used to grade all Intelligence Reports created for this source.

3. Complete CURRENT RISK. – Often begins with ‘NOT YET ASSESSED - (Once the Risk

Assessment is completed, return and select LOW, MEDIUM or HIGH)

4. Complete Community Source. – Yes or No

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 26 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

5. For Personal Details add a search argument in the blue fields. (You will need to the

field every time you ‘Save’ or return to the Source Profile screen as the fields are ‘collapsed’ by default for security purposes.)

Blue fields are ‘search’ fields only

and you must click

6. Once you add your search argument, click

7. Choose Option 1 or 2.

Option 1 – You see a match listed, click

against your chosen entry and you will be

returned to the Source Profile. (Go to Step

13)

Option 2 - If you do not see a

match listed, click

at the bottom of the screen.

This displays the Nominal Details screen.

The ‘Nominal Details’ screen is a

smaller sized panel that contains a lot

of information about a person.

The mandatory fields will already

contain the search name you entered

earlier.

8. If required add multiple

names/spelling by selecting

(eg: you can add a ‘Nickname’)

Note - any details added to ‘Notes’ will

also appear in future printed Performance Indicators and may be viewed and considered as part of any reward request when reviewed by HSMU or REAC.

9. It is optional to complete Nationality, Reference Numbers, Passports, Languages, Licenses,

Appearance, Hair, Marks/Scars/Tattoos/Piercing, Garments, Jewellery Items, and Vulnerability. (not shown here)

10. For adding Prison History, Arrest History, Court Apps, and Warnings, select the relevant

option/s from the top menu bar. (Not all may be accessible in WAPOL)

11. Regardless with what you selected in Step 10, in the next screen select the ‘New’ button.

Depending on what you selected in Step 10 it will take you to the relevant form to complete.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 27 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 REMEMBER - Whenever returning to the Source Profile from a smaller screen like the Nominal Details, ALWAYS click otherwise you will lose your data and will have to re-enter it.

12. In a similar manner as per Steps 7, 8, and 12, add mandatory data for Address Details and

Contact Details, followed by optional data for Vehicles, Family Members, and Employment (You will need to these fields every time you

‘Save’ or return to the Source Profile screen as the fields are ‘collapsed’ by default for security purposes.)

REMEMBER: Blue fields are ‘search’ fields. It is

OK to have empty blue fields. Do not just fill them

for the sake of it with ‘Unknown’ or ‘Not

applicable’; however, if you do add/change data

to it you must click

The System will then search and return

any matches you are permitted to see. If

you see the required address listed, click

When you have finished the Source Profile you shouldn’t see any black typing in blue fields.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 28 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

13. Return to Step 12 until all mandatory information relating to Address Details and Contact

Details are completed, followed by optional information for Vehicles, Family Members, and Employment Details has been added.

14. Complete the Handling Team Details including their training and vetting levels, complying with

the minimum requirements listed in the HSMM.

The District, Station, and Handler details are automatically populated with whoever creates the New Registration. If these are incorrect, please contact the Central Registrar to have them amended.

Mandatory fields exist for a Co-Handler and Supervisor, who must be in the same District and Station as the Handler. Handlers or Supervisors can only see a registration when they are listed on the Source Profile.

It is optional to add multiple (as many as required) Co-Handlers and Deputy Supervisors. To add multiple, click

15. Complete Part B,

Source Capability, Crime

Priority(Mandatory),

Geographical Areas (Optional),

and Motives (Mandatory).

16. Complete Part C, for ‘Lifestyle -

Hobbies, Skills & Memberships’,

‘Places Frequented’,

‘Education’, ‘Associates’, and

‘Other Details’.

Always click Information to

assist when required.

17. Once completing any of these fields, Click Save and Close to return to the Source

Profile

sections.h:
Authorisations vary depending on the circumstances that you select in the wizard.

There are two types, the “Normal” Authorisation for Combined Use and Conduct. This is the only type available with a new registration. Once a Registration becomes “Active”, then a “Authorisation for Participation” becomes available and is only required when a Controlled Operation Authority is used.

The Authorisation for Combined Use and Conduct has three variations

1. Option 1 - “Normal” Authorisation for Combined Use and Conduct – Most Popular, allows

for ongoing management of a Source

2. Option 2 - One-Off Information Capture – This is used when a Source is only expected to

give information on one occasion. It requires the same effort by the Handler as a Normal Authorisation (see previous), but the Supervisor can Authorise a One-Off Information Capture without the Central Registrar being involved. Once Authorised the Registration becomes “Active” but defaults to “Inactive” after 5 days. If circumstances change and the Source needs to be registered for longer, a “normal Authorisation for Combined Use and Conduct must be submitted.

3. Option 3 - Urgent Authorisations -To obtain a Verbal (Urgent) Authority, the process

creates more work for the Handler, so it is recommended you only commence an urgent Authorisation if essential and justified after calling the Central Registrar. An Urgent Verbal Authority only lasts 72 HOURS and must be followed up with a normal Authorisation for Combined use and Conduct before the urgent one expires.

CREATE AN AUTHORISATION – (HANDLER)

sections.t: You Must Now Measure and Record the Levels of Risk
sections.h:
The Handler identifies and records all relevant risk considerations and control measures of
the above five categories.
It is then necessary to MEASURE THE LEVELS OF RISK by the simple calculation of
(Consequences) x (Likelihood) = (Low, Medium, High, Extreme) (Eg 2x4= Medium)
Grading the Risk
A combination of the above likelihood and consequences will equate to a numerical score
which equates to the below grades
1-5 - Low - should be handled by L2 trained handler.
6-9 - Medium - should be handled by L2 trained handler.
10-14 - High - should be handled by L3 trained handler.
15-25 - Extreme - should not be utilised
Qualitative Risk Analysis Matrix - Level of Risk
Qualitative Measures of Likelihood
1 - Rare - May occur only in exceptional circumstances
2 - Unlikely - Could occur at some time
3 - Moderate - Might occur at some time
4 - Likely - Will probably occur in most circumstances
5 - Almost Certain - Expected to occur in most circumstances
FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act
1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You
must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 33
WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025
Qualitative Measures of Consequence or Impact
1 - Insignificant
2 - Minor
3 - Moderate
4 - Major
5 – Catastrophic
Qualitative Measures of Consequence or Impact Descriptions
1 - Insignificant
• (A) Injury - Nil.
• (B) Financial Loss – Corporate - <$50,000 or 0.025% of operational budget
• (C) Financial Loss – Local - 0.025% of operational budget
• (D) Interruption of Services - < 1 hour
• (E) Reputation Image - Unsubstantiated low impact, low profile or no news item
• (F) Operational Efficiency – little impact
• (G) Breach of Procedure/Order/Law – Managerial Intervention Model
2 - Minor
• (A) Injury – First Aid required
• (B) Financial Loss – Corporate - <$250,000 or 0.15% of operational budget
• (C) Financial Loss – Local - < 0.15% of operational budget
• (D) Interruption of Services – 1 hour to 1 day
• (E) Reputation Image - Substantiated, low impact, low news profile
• (F) Operational Efficiency - Inconvenient delays
• (G) Breach of Procedure/Order/Law – Substandard Performance Management
3 - Moderate
• (A) Injury – Medical treatment required
• (B) Financial Loss – Corporate - <$3 million or 2% of operational budget
• (C) Financial Loss – Local - < 2% of operational budget
• (D) Interruption of Services - 1 day to 1 week
• (E) Reputation Image - Substantiated, public embarrassment, moderate impact, moderate news profile
• (F) Operational Efficiency - Delays in major deliverables
• (G) Breach of Procedure/Order/Law - Disciplinary charge
4 - Major
• (A) Injury - Death, Extensive injuries
• (B) Financial Loss – Corporate - <$10 million or 6% of operational budget
• (C) Financial Loss – Local - < 6% of operational budget
• (D) Interruption of Services - 1 week to 1 month
• (E) Reputation Image - Substantiated, public embarrassment, high impact, high news profile, third party
actions
• (F) Operational Efficiency - Non-achievement of major deliverables
• (G) Breach of Procedure/Order/Law – Statutory charge
5 - Catastrophic
• (A) Injury – Multiple deaths, severe permanent disablements
• (B) Financial Loss – Corporate - >$10 million or 6% of operational budget
• (C) Financial Loss – Local - > 6% of operational budget
• (D) Interruption of Services - > 1 month
• (E) Reputation Image - Substantiated, public embarrassment, very high multiple impacts, high
widespread multiple news profile, third party actions
FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act
1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You
must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 34
WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025
• (F) Operational Efficiency - Non-achievement of major key objectives
• (G) Breach of Procedure/Order/Law - Dismissal, Commissioner’s loss of confidence
10. Once you have completed your Risk Assessment, click Save
11. To continue, click SOURCE PROFILE
The AUTHORISATIONS button now shows a ‘document icon’

needed to create a new one.

2. Click Link/Unlink . The Risk Assessment document is now linked.

sections.h:
Once the Registration has been sent to the Supervisor, the Handler will have access to the T& C button to create Terms and Conditions from the Source Profile screen. (Only available when the process state is NEW REGISTRATION SENT TO SUPERVISOR)

In most situations a memo signed by the Source will be required to acknowledge their understanding that the contents of any Letter of Recognition will be divulged to their Lawyer. To prepare the template for printing follow steps 1 to 11. The memo should be signed when the Terms and Conditions are signed.

1. From the Source Profile, Click Documents

2. You will see the Document Production list (probably empty with a new registration)

3. Click NEW

The Document Production process state is NEW RECORD

4. In the mandatory Template field select DIVULGE to LAWYER – Acknowledgment – For signing by HS

5. Click Auto Upload

The Document Production process state is COMPLETED

6. Click in the ATTACHMENTS button .

7. You will see the listed Attachment

8. Click ATTACHMENT

9. A small window appears with the option to Open File

10. Click Open File . If you did not see the small window, navigate to your downloads folder and

double click on it to open it.

11. Print the Document ready for signing.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 36 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

12. Return to the Source Profile Screen by clicking Close three times

13. From the Source Profile screen click then

The Terms and Conditions process state is NEW RECORD

14. Add Date/Time, and any Special Instructions/Comments

15. Click. DO NOT click (You will need to add data, after the meeting/signing)

16. Click Print

17. In the Print Selection screen provide the reason for printing (Signing of Terms and Conditions)

18. Click “Do you want to open or save this file?”

19. Click Open

20. From within the Adobe pdf window click Print

21. Close the pdf document by clicking close.

The Terms and Conditions process state is still NEW RECORD

TERMS AND CONDITIONS – POST MEETING – (HANDLER)

After the Source has signed the Terms and Conditions further attention is required with the hard copy Terms and Conditions and complete the electronic Terms and Conditions

1. Scan the signed document/s giving each file a logical title.

Eg: SIGNED TERMS & CONDITIONS SOURCE URN 123 - 1 Page 1 and/or DIVULGE to LAWYER – Acknowledgment

2. Attach the scanned signed Terms and Conditions and Divulge to Lawyer Acknowledgement to

the Source Profile. (See ATTACHMENTS)

3. Confirm both scanned documents are definitely secured within ACOS, and only then you may

shred the original documents.

4. From Source Profile document click T&C.

5. Click VIEW

The Terms and Conditions process state is

6. Endorse the Date and Print Name fields to reflect the details on the scanned/attached version.

7. Click because you have completed the ‘Terms & Conditions’ process.

The Terms and Conditions process state is COMPLETED

In the mandatory Template field select DIVULGE to LAWYER – Acknowledgment – For signing by HS 5. Click Auto Upload The Document Production process state is COMPLETED 6. Click in the ATTACHMENTS button . 7. You will see the listed Attachment 8. Click ATTACHMENT 9. A small window appears with the option to Open File 10. Click Open File . If you did not see the small window, navigate to your downloads folder and double click on it to open it. 11. Print the Document ready for signing. From within the Adobe pdf window click Print 21. Close the pdf document by clicking close. The Terms and Conditions process state is still NEW RECORD TERMS AND CONDITIONS – POST MEETING – (HANDLER) After the Source has signed the Terms and Conditions further attention is required with the hard copy Terms and Conditions and complete the electronic Terms and Conditions 1. Scan the signed document/s giving each file a logical title. Eg: SIGNED TERMS & CONDITIONS SOURCE URN 123 - 1 Page 1 and/or DIVULGE to LAWYER – Acknowledgment 2. Attach the scanned signed Terms and Conditions and Divulge to Lawyer Acknowledgement to the Source Profile. (See ATTACHMENTS) 3. Confirm both scanned documents are definitely secured within ACOS, and only then you may shred the original documents. 4. From Source Profile document click T&C. 5. Click VIEW The Terms and Conditions process state is 6. Endorse the Date and Print Name fields to reflect the details on the scanned/attached version. 7. sections.h:
Any planned contact between a Handler and a Source must be documented by the Handler in a Meeting Request and authorised by the Supervisor. Meeting Requests can only be done retrospectively if the supervisor has been advised of the intention to contact prior to it taking place.

No meeting Request is required when the Source initiates an unplanned meeting or Contact. One Meeting Request may be acceptable to cover multiple planned contacts in 1 calendar day only if approved by the Supervisor.

A meeting request can be created from multiple locations. Choose the correct option.

Option 1 - MEETING REQUEST CREATED FROM A TASKING REQUEST If you have created a tasking request you can create a meeting request from the tasking request if they relate to the same planned meeting event. You are prompted to link the Meeting request as you create it.

1. From the Tasking Request form click

2. Click

3. Nominate a reason for the Meeting.

4. Click

The Meeting Request process state is

5. Fill in the form as appropriate, paying particular attention to the mandatory boxes.

6. Use the buttons to add multiple dates/times and reasons.

7. If the meeting request is verbally authorised complete the details of the person authorising and

the time and date the meeting was authorised. The details are selected from the LOV if the person is in the list otherwise the details may be entered as free text.

8. Click , NOTIFY , then SEND

The Meeting Request process state is

Option 2 - MEETING REQUEST CREATED FROM A SOURCE PROFILE You can create a Meeting Request from the Source Profile, but once it is created you may need to manually link it to a Tasking Request if there was a need to create one.

1. From the Source Profile click

2. Click

The Meeting Request process state is

3. Fill in the form as appropriate, paying particular attention to the mandatory boxes.

4. Use the buttons to add multiple dates/times and reasons.

2. View the meeting details and check it has been authorised.

3. View the Supervisor comments regarding the meeting.

sections.h:
14. Complete the “IDM Formatted IR’s” field following these points;

for re-work.

sections.h:
Policy Logs are used to record any decision made or action taken that is not recorded elsewhere.

The ‘Policy Log’ button is readily accessible from numerous screens (eg Source Profile). It does not matter what screen the policy Log is created from, they all appear together for that Registration.

1. From the Source Profile (or other screens) Click

This will open a Policy Log Summary screen showing all existing Policy Logs for the Source.

2. Select

The Policy Log process state is

3. Complete Description (title) with reference to the specific document it relates to. Eg ‘Contact

Sheet (insert #) corroborated by Co-handler’,

4. Provide Decision made, and the Rational on how/why the decision was made.

5. Endorse ‘Policy Made By’, Date Made, and Access Level.

Assess Level identifies who can see this Policy Log. It defaults to your own level of access.

You can restrict the level equal to or below your own level. E.g. a Handler can’t change it because they have the lowest level of access, but other user levels can.

In most cases the access level should be set to Handler so all users can view the decision made.

You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 42 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

6. You have two options,

A. Set a review date before you click Complete.

This sends a message to the Handler, Supervisor, and CR) on the expiry date. The Reviewer can make comment and/or create another review date or tick the ‘Review Completed’ option.

Reviewing the policy log numerous times functions like a running sheet.

B. There are no further actions required, you just wanted to record a decision or action a. Click and

The Policy Log process state is

SINGLE or STAGED REWARDS - (HANDLER)

For accountability, all Intelligence used to justify rewards must be recorded in ACOS (in Contacts Sheets and Sanitised Intelligence Reports). If Intelligence is not recorded in ACOS it cannot be used in a reward application.

sections.h:
Performance Indicators are used to record the results from information provided by the Source.

They include Crimes Detected, Arrests, and Recovered items (drugs, firearms, vehicles, property).

Performance Indicators may be completed from the Reward Request screen or the Source Summary Screen.

If the Performance Indicator is created from the Reward, it will be automatically linked to that Reward.

Only one set of Performance Indicators is required for each Reward Application.

1. Open the required Reward Request

2. Click . You will see the Performance Indicator Summary Screen.

3. Click . You will see a new ‘Performance Indicators Record’

The Performance Indicators Record process state is

4. Complete as many fields as are relevant, press the ‘add’ to create a new multi row block

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 44 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 When adding Arrests, the process is the same as the adding the Nominal Details in the Source Profile screen, the ‘Notes’ in the persons Nominal Details will automatically transpose into the Performance Indicators

5. Record all IMS Incident Report Numbers that relate to the reward application.

6. For Arrests add a search argument in the blue fields and click

This will display a screen showing any matching results. This will only display a result if the arrested person was previously added to ACOS and you are permitted to see that record.

7. Choose Option 1 or 2.

Option 1 - If you see a match to your search,

click ‘Edit’, against your chosen entry

Option 2 - If you do not see a suitable search result, click ‘Create New Nominal’ at the bottom of the screen.

8. This displays the Nominal Details screen.

sections.h:
A Working Expense is not a reward, but is accessed in ACOS from under the Reward button.

Working Expenses must have prior approval from the Central Registrar A working expense is not to be utilised as a pseudo reward. Working expenses must be attributable to specific tasking but if approved by the Central Registrar, can be used for rapport building.

The below table provides examples of acceptable working expenses;

Communications Mobile phone / sim card purchase & phone charge credit. (Note this includes sim card purchase as a point of contact for Source).

Travel Fuel, taxi fares, public transport and in exceptional circumstances, vehicle maintenance or flights.

Accommodation Hotel room, holiday park bookings and associated costs (to cover Source - target meetings and tasking).

Sustenance Meals, beverages (for the source only)

Sundry Drivers licence renewal, fines enforcement (extreme circumstances), rapport Items/Payments enhancing gift (to be approved by the Central Registrar) social club / membership fees related to tasking or long-term deployment of the Source.

sections.h: The HANDLER must initiate a request for a Letter of Recognition (LOR) in ACOS ASAP.
sections.b: The HANDLER must initiate a request for a Letter of Recognition (LOR) in ACOS ASAP.

sections.h:
The Letter of Recognition request is commenced by the Handler to inform the HSMU that there is a need for a Letter of Recognition to be written and submitted on the Handler’s behalf.

The Handler must also arrange for a ‘Lawyers Letter’ to be signed by the Source in which the Source acknowledges that Defence Counsel will be advised of the contents of any LoR that is used in the sentencing process. The Source is to be made aware of their lawyer being advised of the letter before any intelligence is requested by the handling team.

1. From the Source Profile click then new

2. Select Letter of Recognition, OK, and Next

The Letter Request process state is New Record

3. Complete the mandatory field.

4. Click Notify and Send

The Letter Request process state is

The notification is sent to the Central Registrar Intel Cell (LOR Writers) who will respond by commencing the LoR process.

It is the HANDLERS responsibility to keep the Letter Writers informed of any new information or changes that may affect the due date or content of the LOR.

Please ensure all court Dates are kept up to date in the LOR application.

TIME CRITICAL –The HSMU Letter Writers can only compose an accurate LOR if given all the above information in a timely manner giving sufficient time for the writers to compile, seek A/C Authorisation, and deliver the letter/s to the court on time on your behalf.

So, this form MUST be commenced and sent to the Supervisor ASAP after you become aware the Source has requested and/or is entitled to an LOR.

If the LOR is required within 14 days you MUST contact the HSMU URGENTLY. Once an LOR is AUTHORISED by the A/C no further changes or additions can be made.

sections.h:
REVIEW – (HANDLER)

A review of each ‘Active’ Registration is mandatory at regular intervals.

The Authorisation form for Adult Registrations must be reviewed every 3 months.

The Authorisation form for Juvenile Registrations must be reviewed every 14 days.

The Handler must respond to system notifications informing them that a review of the Authorisation form is due in 14 days and the Review must be submitted before the due date.

sections.h:
‘Forwarding’ from the button, sends messages to other users.

‘Forwarding’ is similar to the ‘Notification’ process except;

- No pre-defined recipients exist, so users will need to manually add the recipient/s.

- Forwarding does not alter the ‘process state’ at the bottom of the document.

1. From the required document Click (top of right-hand menu).

A new notification screen will open.

2. To add a Recipient, click

The system only lists the users permitted to see the related document so if a name does not appear then that user does not have permission to view the related document. Repeat if multiple recipients are required.

3. Optional - add a message in the ‘User Notification Text’ field.

4. Optional - Under ‘Return If Unread In’, enter a number of hours after which, if the recipient

hasn’t read the message, you will receive a notification.

5. If the notification requires urgent attention tick the ‘Time Critical’ box

Time Critical notifications appear highlighted in red and remain on top of the recipient’s inbox.

6. Click The notification will be forwarded to the listed recipient’s

‘Forwarding’ does not change the process state of the document.

If you later need confirmation that you have sent the notification, go to the home screen and check your Sent Items.

AUTHORISATION for PARTICIPATION – (HANDLER)

An Authorisation for Participation must be obtained for a Source who is subject to a Controlled Operation Authority.

The Participation Authorisation form is only available once the Source Profile is ‘Active’.

Additions to the Risk Assessment are asked for in the Application. (No need for a separate Risk Assessment)

1. From the Source Profile, click then

2. In the wizard select ‘Authorisation for Participation’ (Only visible when a Source Profile is

‘Active’).

3. Click

4. Select the relevant need for urgency. It will usually be Non-Urgent as Urgent Authorisations

are only done when the operational need for urgency occurs and discussion with the Supervisor confirms this. Urgent Authorisations are more work and serve little purpose in the WAPOL environment, as the Controlled Operation Authority is the legislative requirement and ACOS supports that Authority.

For Urgent Authorisations go to Step 5

For Non-Urgent Authorisations go to Step 6

5. Urgent Authorisations - To obtain a Verbal (Urgent) Authority, the Handler must:

A. Consult with the proposed Co-handler and Supervisor and establish a genuine need for an Urgent Verbal Authority. (Only lasts 72 Hours)

B. Phone the Central Registrar and request an Urgent Verbal Authority.

C. Be well prepared to provide sufficient details so the Central Registrar can make a solid determination.

D. Once the Central Registrar approves the Urgent Authorisation and you select that option you will be prompted to record who and what time the Urgent Authorisation was granted YOU NOW HAVE ONLY 72 HOURS to complete a normal Authorisation for Participation before the Urgent Authorisation for Participation expires. (Remember this process requires action by Handler, Supervisor, and Central Registrar within the 72 Hours.) If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. The Participation Authorisation process state is

9. Click

10. View the latest (Top) Risk Assessment listed, and identify any further risks for inclusion when

completing the Authorisation for Participation form in Step12.

11. Once happy that it is the latest and most suitable Risk assessment to link to the Authorisation

for Participation, close it and tick the link box then click link then

12. Complete the form using the information buttons provided and ensuring you list New Risks

identified and the New Control Measures.

13. Attach (when available) a copy of, (1) Controlled Operation Application, (2) Controlled Operation

Authority, and (3) the Civilian Participation Undertaking The Participation Authorisation process state is

14. Click then (Authorisation for Participation)

The Participation Authorisation process state is

The process continues up the chain-of-command to Supervisor, then to the Central Registrar and (if authorised) returns ‘Authorised’ to the Handler.

sections.h:
The Central Registrar will not accept requests for information unless they are submitted via the formal RFI process in ACOS. This ensures auditable records of any request and controls the dissemination of information.

The RFI request is sent to the Central Registrar who can conduct searches and return the required details if available, or create a Batch Tasking Request in an attempt to obtain specific intelligence not yet obtained from multiple Sources.

All information released by the Central Registrar via the RFI process must have Assistant Commissioner Approval.

sections.h:
A review of each ‘Active’ Registration is mandatory at regular intervals.

The Authorisation form for Adult Registrations must be reviewed every 3 months.

The Authorisation form for Juvenile Registrations must be reviewed every 14 days.

The Handler commences a Review and a notification should be received by the Supervisor well before the due date. If a Review has not been processed before the due date then an ‘overdue’ warning is sent to all users.

1. From the Home screen open the relevant message for the Review.

2. Click

The Review process state is

3. Check the document and complete the required fields

4. Click Save (‘Officer completing’ and ‘Date/time’ fields are automatic)

Reviews require a Risk Assessment.

5. Click to review the Risk Assessment

6. Click VIEW. (next to the linked Risk Assessment)

The process state will either be or

7. If the Risk Assessment process state is it means it is a pre-existing Risk

Assessment and needs no further attention because it is still 100% suitable (Go to Step 21)

8. If the Risk Assessment process state is it means a new Risk

Assessment was created by the Handler and it now needs to be authorised.

9. See RISK ASSESSMENT AUTHORISATION - (SUPERVISOR)

‘Risk Assessments’ and ‘Reviews’ require similar attention so do not get them confused.

10. Click Close twice to return to the Review form.

11. The Review process state is

sections.h:
PROCESSES

ACOS has tiered authorisation levels for financial payments (rewards or expenses) depending on the value of the request.

The Rewards Evaluation Advisory Committee (REAC) will be responsible for the management of all reward applications that are above $1000 or where rewards are sort from Government based rewards schemes i.e. Arson Rewards Scheme.

Rewards can be created from active and inactive registrations.

PROCESSING REWARD REQUESTS – (SUPERVISOR)

The only difference between Single and Staged Payments are a Single Payment has a single payment and Staged Payments have multiple payments.

1. The Supervisor opens the Request by opening the attachment.

The Reward Request process state is

2. Review the request including the Amount requested, Staged Payment Plan (for Staged

Payment only), BACKGROUND INFORMATION, the matrix settings, Circumstances, Other Rewards and supporting documentation. (Contact Sheets, Sanitised IR’s and Performance Indicators)

It is vital the ‘Circumstances’ content is accurate and factual. All listed names, charges, dates, weights and amounts must be the same as listed in the Performance Indicators. You are about to approve something that will be viewed by REAC members

3. Complete the Supervisor’s Comments field.

4. Tab to add your details and date, click

5. You have a choice to ‘Authorise’, ‘Return for Rework’ or ‘Reject’.

Option 1- If the Reward Request is accurate and you are sure the content and quality of the request submitted by the Handler is suitable, click

(Notification automatically sent to Central Registrar) and you will see this alert The Reward Request process state will be

Options 2 - If the Reward Request requires more work by the Handler click (You will be prompted to comment and send a Notification to Handler and Co-Handler).

The Reward Request process state will be

Option 3 - If the request is not to be progressed, click (Notification sent to Handler and Co-Handler).

If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 66 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 The Reward Request process state will be

1. From the Notification open the attachment

The Payment Request, process state is

2. If all details are correct click

The Payment Request, process state is.

If the payment has not been paid, you have no action, but simply to be informed of the state.

sections.h:
The Letter of Recognition request is to alert the Central Registrar that a Letter of Recognition will be required. The Supervisor is included in the process so they are aware of the request. They are required to endorse the Request even though it has already arrived at the CR LOV Writers mailbox and may already be commenced.

The Handler creates a Request for a Letter of Recognition and notifies the Supervisor.

1. The Supervisor opens the Request notification clicking on the

The Letter Request process state is

2. Review the request and make comment in the Supervisor’s Comments field.

3. You have a choice three options, ‘Approve’, ‘Return for Rework’ or ‘Reject’.

4. If you wish to progress the request, click

(Notification automatically sent to Central Registrar Intel Cell) The Letter Request process state will be

5. You also have the option to Return/Rework or Reject the request.

If you select these options, you will be prompted to comment why, when the system sends a Notification to the Handler and Co-Handler

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 69 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

STEP BY STEP – CENTRAL REGISTRAR

REGISTRATION – APPROVAL - (CENTRAL REGISTRAR)

The Handler creates a Source Profile, Authorisation and a Risk Assessment. The Supervisor reviews the documents, approves the Risk Assessment and notifies the Central Registrar. The Central Registrar will be automatically notified if the system detects a duel registration, allowing the Central Registrar to deal with the situation as required.

1. Select Notification titled ‘Approved by HSM (Supervisor), sent to Central Registrar

2. Click

The Source Profile process state is

3. Review the Source Profile and linked documents including the Risk Assessment by clicking

on and VIEW the relevant document.

The Risk Assessment process state is

The Risk Assessment was Authorised by the Supervisor. It is optional if the Central Registrar chooses to make a comment.

4. Consider optional comments in the ‘Central Registrar's Comments’ field.

5. If comments are added click

6. If further work is required on the Risk Assessment, Click

This will revert the whole registration Including Source Profile, Authorisation, and Risk Assessment back to

7. If the Risk Assessment is suitable, Click then VIEW the Authorisation for

Combined Use and Conduct.

The Authorisation process state is

Very rarely the process state could be if the Authorisation has at some stage been returned for rework. This is evident if the Comments button has a document attached to it.

8. Complete the mandatory field ‘Central Registrar's comments for Authorisation or

Rejection’,

9. Press the Tab key to populate all the endorsement and date fields.

10. Click

11. You have two options (A) or (B) the Application.

12. Option (A) Click and follow prompts.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 70 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 A notification is sent to Handler’s and Supervisor.

The Authorisation process state is

The Source Profile process state is .

13. Option (B) Click and ‘OK’ when prompted.

A notification is sent to Handler/s and Supervisor

The Authorisation process state is

14. Click

The Source Profile process state is

AUTHORISATION APPROVAL– WITHIN 72 Hrs of URGENT REG - (CR) Within 72 hours of the Central Registrar verbally authorising an Urgent Registration, the Handler creates another Authorisation for Combined Use and Conduct along with a linked or unique Risk Assessment. The Handler notifies the Central Registrar.

1. Via the Home Screen the Central Registrar opens the new message titled ‘Approved by HSM

(Supervisor), sent to Central Registrar’.

2. Click on the attachment.

The Authorisation process state is

3. Follow the relevant steps in REGISTRATION – APPROVAL - (CENTRAL REGISTRAR)

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 71 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

REWARD–SINGLE/STAGED PAYMENT – (CENTRAL REGISTRAR)

The Handler creates a Reward Request and sends it to the Supervisor who approves it causing a notification to be sent to the Central Registrar.

The Central Registrar is the ‘safe hands’ for the ‘REAC pack’ to the REAC).

1. From the Home Screen view the Central Registrar Inbox.

2. View the relevant Notification.

3. Click

The Reward Request process state is

4. From the Reward Request, review the Contact Sheets, and Sanitised IR’s and Performance

Indicators. Performance indicators can only be ‘Completed’ by the CR or CR Intel

5. Ensure the ‘BACKGROUND INFORMATION ’ and REASONS FOR MATRIX SELECTIONS

fields are accurate. All listed names, charges, dates, weights and amounts must be the same as listed in the Prosecution details and the Performance Indicators.

6. If you are printing the Request form for the Authorising Officer or REAC chairperson to hand

write comments, then expand the AUTHORISING OFFICER DECISION field by entering one keystroke eg (.) and fifteen returns (enter). This will expand the field when printed so the REAC chairperson has a space to write a comment.

7. Click

8. Print the REAC PACK by clicking Print . This will display the Print Selection screen.

Note - the Report From and Report To fields will auto populate and do not show-up on printed documents.

9. Add a ‘Reason for Print’ – In this case ‘CR Reward Pack’.

10. Selecting documents for printing

Option 1 – Print multiple documents

Tick the documents you wish to print, or alternatively click Click

Option 2 – Print individual documents

Click the button of a document.

11. This will create one pdf document.

12. You can now print the pdf document.

Central Registrar Role – ‘Safe Hands’ Once the REAC process is completed and approved and the AC Authorisation has been obtained, the ‘hard copy’ signed Reward Request form needs to be scanned and uploaded onto the system.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 72 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

13. Complete the AUTHORISING OFFICER DECISION field electronically to accurately reflect the

signed/ scanned document and endorse it with the Authorising Officers Details

14. Click or

If rejected, the Reward Request process state is

If authorised, the Reward Request process state is

Notifications are automatically sent to the Handlers and Supervisor.

15. The option to the Authorisation is now available for the Central Registrar if required.

WORKING EXPENSE – (CENTRAL REGISTRAR)

A Handler creates a Payment Request for a Working Expense and after the Supervisor attempts to authorise it, it will usually be escalated to the Central Registrar.

1. From the Home Screen open the notification titled ‘Reward Request awaiting authorisation’

2. Open attachment

The Payment Request process state is

3. Review the request including the Amount requested, and supporting comments.

4. Make a comment in the ‘AUTHORISING OFFICER DECISION’ field. This may relate to any

direction given by a higher authority.

5. Add the mandatory ‘Amount Authorised’. (If changed from the requested amount a

confirmation prompt will appear.)

6. If the authority came from another person, amend the ‘Request Authorised By’ field with their

credentials

7. Click

8. Choose between three options, AUTHORISE, RETURN/REWORK, or REJECT.

Option 1- If the Payment Request is accurate and suitable, click The Payment Request process state is

Option 2 - If the Payment Request requires more work by the Handler click (You will be prompted to comment and send a notification to Handler).

The Payment Request process state will be

Option 3 - If the request is not suitable and must never be progressed, click (Notification sent to Handler)

The Payment Request process state will be

If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 73 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025

sections.h:
The Handler creates an Authorisation for Participation along with a linked or unique Risk Assessment. The Handler notifies the Supervisor who actions the application and notifies the Central Registrar.

1. From the Home Screen open Central Registrar Inbox.

2. Select the forwarded notification ‘Authorisation for Participation viewed by Supervisor’

3. Click

4. The Participation process state is

5. Review the Source Profile and linked documents including the Risk Assessment (if applicable)

by clicking on and VIEW the relevant document.

6. Back in the Authorisation for Participation form, add a Central Registrar’s Comment

7. Click

8. Click or and OK if prompted.

Known bug – when the Central Registrar clicks ‘Authorise’ the prompt may appear stating a Participation Terms and Conditions needs to be created. If this occurs click ‘PART T & C’ and then close it again. This will create a blank Participation Terms and Conditions and allow the process to continue. Now click ‘Authorise’ again.

The Participation process state is

If the Participation was rejected then the process state is Notifications sent to Handler/s and Supervisor.

sections.h:
Any User can submit an RFI, which is then sent direct to the Central Registrar Intel Cell for actioning.

1. From Central Registrar Share Inbox, open the relevant Notification

2. Click

The Request for Information process state is

3. Either conduct a search of the system to locate the requested information, or if a Batch Task is

required, (See BATCH TASKING)

4. As a result of a Search or a Batch Task, you will have a positive or negative result.

FREEDOM OF INFORMATION - This document may contain information which is exempt matter for the purposes of the Freedom of Information Act 1992 (FOI Act). If you receive a FOI Act request seeking access to this document you must immediately notify the Commander – State Crime. You must not release or disseminate this document or any part thereof, without the approval of the Assistant Commissioner – State Crime. 74 WAPOL ACOS Source Management Guidelines – Version 1.1 (Compatible with ACOS V 5.5.2.0 & HSMM 3.1) 07/JAN/2025 Any information considered for release must be vetted by the Assistant Commissioner I&C prior to release except when the requestor is from Internal Affairs.

A. Negative result - If a Search or Batch Task results in no result, details must be recorded in the Results Details. How this is done depends on who the requestor is.

- If the requestor is from Internal Affairs all result details should be recorded in the Results

Details field.

- If the results are any other user, then the ‘no result’ wording should be written in a way

that does not declare the existence or non-existence of the information such as. ‘No information is available for this request. (This may mean there is a result but it can not be release or there is no result)

Clicking results in the RFI being returned to the requestor with the Results Details visible for the Handler to read.

The Request for Information process state is

B. Positive Result requiring Assistant Commissioner vetting Record positive results in the Results Details field and click and to send the RFI is sent to the ASSISTANT COMMISSIONER OF I&C for vetting The Request for Information process state is

5. The Assistant Commissioner can Reject or Approve the released of the supplied information.

The Central Registrar also has the option to Reject or Approve on behalf of the Assistant Commissioner

The Central Registrar has received a request for Information that requires Assistant Commissioner of I&C vetting to determine if dissemination is permitted. The Central Registrar has notified the Assistant Commissioner.

1. The Assistant Commissioner will receive a notification advising that an RFI is awaiting

approval.

2. Click on the email Notification.

3. Open the attachment . You will see the RFI request form.

The Request for Information process state is

4. Choose to Approve or Reject the request.

Option 1- Approve –

If the information is to be released in its current state, Approval Details must be completed accordingly.

Click

sections.h:
1. Option 1 – The Request for Information process state is

Ensure only the approved information is in the Results Details, and any information ’not to be released’ is in the Approval Details section.

Click to return the RFI to the originator.

The Request for Information process state is

2. Option 2 - The Request for Information process state is

If the Assistant Commissioner rejects any dissemination of information, the Central Registrar must cut and paste the Results Details into the Approval Details field.

A generic comment is then placed in the Result Details field. This way none of the information will be released to the Requestor.

sections.h:
西澳大利亚警察局

线人保密协议条款和条件

日期 ___________________

西澳大利亚警察局工作人员解释如下：

你同意做西澳大利亚警察局的登记线人，就相关调查帮助警方并提供信息。

重要的一点是，你明白你的线人登记取决于如下条款和条件。你尽管提问题，以确保你明白如下条款和条件:

(1) 任何时候你都不被认为是西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员；

(2) 你不得向任何人透露你的身份。这些人包括你的家人、朋友们或其它执法机关的官员；

(3) 西澳大利亚警察局可能会让你执行任务。如果你同意执行任何任务，你必须按照指示完成任务；

(4) 你如果未经合法授权从事犯罪活动，你的线人登记不能保护你免于被诉讼。本协议不包含法律授权。非法活动包

括煽动、鼓励或协助任何人从事违法活动；

(5) 西澳大利亚警察局将尽全力保护你的身份。但是在特定情况下，法庭可能会行使决定权，在诉讼过程中公开你的

身份；

(6) 如果你在协议期间犯罪，西澳大利亚警察局可能会取消你获得任何经济利益的权利；

(7) 如果你能够获得一份支持信，你的法律代表将在你被判刑前收到通知。如果你的法律代表要求，西澳大利亚警察

局将提供评估的机会（参考备忘录第二页）；

(8) 就你可能提供的信息和服务，西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员没有获得授权来给你任何报酬方面（现金或其它方

式）的承诺和保证；

(9) 你可能会接触到不同的西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员或其他执法机关的工作人员。这些部门可能会要求你提供帮

助；

(10) 西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员对你提供的任何信息，你要严格保密，不得向任何人透露。在特定情况下，透露信

息会构成刑事犯罪；并且

(11) 违背这些条款和条件会使你的线人登记被撤销，也会影响你今后为西澳大利亚警察局提供信息的机会。

我(签名人),明白本协议的条款和条件，并同意做西澳大利亚警察局的登记线人。

sections.b: 西澳大利亚警察局 线人保密协议条款和条件 日期 ___________________ 西澳大利亚警察局工作人员解释如下： 你同意做西澳大利亚警察局的登记线人，就相关调查帮助警方并提供信息。 重要的一点是，你明白你的线人登记取决于如下条款和条件。你尽管提问题，以确保你明白如下条款和条件: (1) 任何时候你都不被认为是西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员； (2) 你不得向任何人透露你的身份。这些人包括你的家人、朋友们或其它执法机关的官员； (3) 西澳大利亚警察局可能会让你执行任务。如果你同意执行任何任务，你必须按照指示完成任务； (4) 你如果未经合法授权从事犯罪活动，你的线人登记不能保护你免于被诉讼。本协议不包含法律授权。非法活动包 括煽动、鼓励或协助任何人从事违法活动； (5) 西澳大利亚警察局将尽全力保护你的身份。但是在特定情况下，法庭可能会行使决定权，在诉讼过程中公开你的 身份； (6) 如果你在协议期间犯罪，西澳大利亚警察局可能会取消你获得任何经济利益的权利； (7) 如果你能够获得一份支持信，你的法律代表将在你被判刑前收到通知。如果你的法律代表要求，西澳大利亚警察 局将提供评估的机会（参考备忘录第二页）； (8) 就你可能提供的信息和服务，西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员没有获得授权来给你任何报酬方面（现金或其它方 式）的承诺和保证； (9) 你可能会接触到不同的西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员或其他执法机关的工作人员。这些部门可能会要求你提供帮 助； (10) 西澳大利亚警察局的工作人员对你提供的任何信息，你要严格保密，不得向任何人透露。在特定情况下，透露信 息会构成刑事犯罪；并且 (11) 违背这些条款和条件会使你的线人登记被撤销，也会影响你今后为西澳大利亚警察局提供信息的机会。 我(签名人),明白本协议的条款和条件，并同意做西澳大利亚警察局的登记线人。 特殊指示/意见 签字 X 拼写名字 警方签字 （会面之后） 警方签字 拼写名字 （组长） （会面之后） 日期 1992 年 （西澳大利亚州）信息自由法 本文件可能包含不受 1992 年(西澳大利亚州)信息自由法 (FOI Act) 制约的内容。如果您收到信息自由法允许获得本文件的请求， 您必须马上通知州犯罪调查局的局长。如果没有获得负 责州犯罪调查局助理警监的批准，您不可以发布或发放本文件以及其中任何内容。 线人保密协议 我（签名人）向西澳大利亚警察局提供了信息。为此，我获得了一 份由西澳大利亚警察局提供的支持信。该文件可以在我被判刑的时 候呈交法庭。 我知道我的律师将获悉该支持信的内容。 签字： ............................ 日期： ........ / ......... / ....... 证人： ............................ 1992 年 （西澳大利亚州）信息自由法 本文件可能包含不受 1992 年(西澳大利亚州)信息自由法 (FOI Act) 制约的内容。如果您收到信息自由法允许获得本文件的请求， 您必须马上通知州犯罪调查局的局长。如果没有获得负 责州犯罪调查局助理警监的批准，您不可以发布或发放本文件以及其中任何内容。

sections.h:
This document is a guide for Inspectors to consider. Knowledge of the source, the operations, the handlers or other circumstances may require consideration as to how invasively to review.

6 Monthly “Admin Review” required for all Tier 2 (Medium Risk) Human Source and to be Policy Logged (refer Human Source Management Manual V3.1 page 11).

To aid Central Registrar (CR) and Corruption and Crime Commission oversight, preferred Policy Log description is: “Inspector XXX - Admin Review - XXX” (i.e., Inspector Great Southern – Admin Review - Minor issues to address) It is good practice to use the notify feature to send a copy in ACOS to the Handler and Human Source Manager. ACOS guides and the HSMM are available on the Human Source Management Unit Bluebook.

sections.h:
MESSAGE

The Western Australia Police Force (WA Police Force) recognises that professional and responsible management of human sources (sources) is an essential and valuable part of the investigative and intelligence gathering process aimed at delivering increased community safety in the form of criminal prosecutions.

It is of paramount importance that safeguards, controls and assessment processes are imposed to provide safe, efficient and effective management of sources.

The WA Police Force Human Source Management Manual (the Manual) provides a comprehensive management structure to identify and control risk while achieving investigative results. The tiered approach to the management of sources is designed to encourage growth in the use of community (non-criminal) source and apply a scalable risk- based governance framework.

Management of sources in line with this manual will reflect the expected behaviours identified in the agency’s Code of Conduct and the agency’s principles of Honesty, Performance, Competence and Conduct.
sections.b: MESSAGE The Western Australia Police Force (WA Police Force) recognises that professional and responsible management of human sources (sources) is an essential and valuable part of the investigative and intelligence gathering process aimed at delivering increased community safety in the form of criminal prosecutions. It is of paramount importance that safeguards, controls and assessment processes are imposed to provide safe, efficient and effective management of sources. The WA Police Force Human Source Management Manual (the Manual) provides a comprehensive management structure to identify and control risk while achieving investigative results. The tiered approach to the management of sources is designed to encourage growth in the use of community (non-criminal) source and apply a scalable risk- based governance framework. Management of sources in line with this manual will reflect the expected behaviours identified in the agency’s Code of Conduct and the agency’s principles of Honesty, Performance, Competence and Conduct.

sections.h:
INTRODUCTION

The WA Police Force uses Human Source in line with The Five Key Investigative Strategies (The5KIS), as stipulated in the WA Police Force Investigation Manual.

Effective management of Human Source enhances the capability of the WA Police Force to solve crimes faster and deliver positive community safety outcomes through timely and quality investigations.

This Human Source Management Manual (Manual) defines the requirements of WA Police Force employees involved in the management of sources. Contravention of the HSM Manual may be subject to criminal, civil or disciplinary measures. If a matter is not covered by the HSM Manual, clarification should be sought from the Central Registrar.

The HSM Manual should be read in conjunction with the ACOS instruction manual and ‘How to Use’ guides that can be obtained from the Central Registrar.

Human Source Handler Level 1 training is available on the WA Police Force ‘eforce’ Learning web site. Human Source Handler Level 1 is the minimum qualification required before involvement in any Human Source management.

Human Source Handler Level 2 and Level 3 training courses are regularly conducted by the Human Source Management Unit (HSMU). Contact the Central Registrar for further information.

The Officer in Charge of the HSMU is the WA Police Force Central Registrar. The Central Registrar, oversighted by the Inspector Crime Support and Services Division, has overall authority regarding the management of Human Sources by employees of the WA Police Force.

The identity of a Human Source must be protected at all times:

• never make oral reference to the Human Source by name.

• never print the name of a Human Source on any document (Exception - Letter of

Recognition and Terms & Conditions).

WA Police Force employees should not rely upon Public Interest Immunity (PII) as a mechanism to restrict access to information stored within ACOS.

ACOS

The ACOS system must be used to document the management of Human Sources. ACOS assists in the management and deployment of Human Sources and the recording and sharing of intelligence.

Unrestricted ACOS access is limited to:

• the Central Registrar and HSMU staff assisting the Central Registrar;

• the Inspector of the Crime Support & Services Division (oversight of the Central

Registrar); and

• designated positions of the Internal Affairs Unit.

Unrestricted access to persons outside of the above parameters requires the approval of the Commander, State Crime.

The Human Source management process is designed to:

• safeguard the Human Source through the application of appropriate risk management

processes and strategies;

• identify High Risk Human Source, ensuring appropriate risk assessments are

undertaken prior to registration;

• ensure appropriate actions are undertaken to protect the identity of a Human Source

whilst being aware that in certain circumstances, the court may exercise discretion and require the identity of the Human Source to be revealed;

• maximise the benefits to WA Police Force and the community of information obtained;

• protect WA Police Force employees from false allegations of corruption or impropriety

arising out of dealings with Human Sources;

• provide objective supervision and assessment of the relationship between WA Police

employees and Human Source;

• ensure the correct application for payments, whether monetary or otherwise, are

appropriately dealt with in accordance with the Manual and other guidelines that are associated with the reward/benefit processes;

• ensure management and expenditure of working expenses is in accordance with this

Manual; and

• prevent unwarranted duplication of Human Source registrations.
