sections.h:
Western Australia Police Force State Intelligence

Tasking Request Form

Freedom of Information

This document is an exempt document under the Freedom of Information Act (WA) 1992. Section 5(2) of Schedule 1 applies to the document. Refer requests to Director, State Intelligence, Western Australia Police Force.

Request Details

Ensure you select your own district and team.

Do not select an operation from the drop-down list unless it is the operation you are working from.

If you do not have an operation name then you will need to create an operation prior to submitting the

TRF.

Do not use overarching PD/LBS operations for requests for covert resources, you will need to create a new operation for this type of request.

Offence / Criminal Activity / Event / Incident

Child sex offences - s.321(4) & (8) Criminal Code

Homicide - Murder s.279 Criminal Code

Money Laundering, Possession of a prohibited drug with Intent to Sell or Supply.

Importing trafficable quantities of illicit commodities into Western Australia and the associated money laundering.

Multiple cross district volume crime offences

SID Resource Requirements (indicate if required)

Select the resources you require to achieve your current objectives and strategy. Multiple services can be requested per TRF, but the services must be reasonably required at that time.

Do not cast the net over all resources just in case you might need them in the future. If you do require additional resources further down the line then a new TRF will be required at that time.

1 Requesting Officer Particulars

2 Person / Group / Address of Interest

One TRF per target.

Confirm spelling of names and date of birth match IMS.

3 Background and analysis completed

This section is for an overview of the background and current status of the investigation. This doesn't need to be lengthy and only requires a few paragraphs.

Please limit methodology, human source, or intelligence disclosure unless absolutely necessary.

We’d rather see “information obtained indicates” as opposed to “HS1234 says..”, “during intelligence operation 1AB..” or “UCO’s have conducted 3 buys from..”

Do not record names of witnesses or victims and refer to them as such.

Example: On 18/JAN/2024 information received suggests the suspect (INSERT NAME) is involved in drug importation with an associate (INSERT NAME).

Approximately 25 kilograms is due to land in WA on 03/MAR/2024.

It is unknown how the drugs are being imported and it is suggested (INSERT NAME) and (INSERT NAME) will not go hands on and will utilise unknown runners. IDM IR 20240017199 refers.

On 20/JAN/2024 information was received that (INSERT NAME) is involved in a drug importation due to land in WA on 03/FEB/2024 on behalf of the commission in the Eastern States.

The commission is believed to send around 100 kilograms of methylamphetamine a month. IDM IR 20240019523 refers.

Information also indicates that on 03/FEB/2024, the ECN landed approximately 350 kilograms of methylamphetamine.

Information suggests that on Tuesday 06/FEB/2024, a member of the ECN (INSERT NAME) will be given a hire car and new mobile phone and tasked to drive approximately 4 hours south of Perth WA to inspect and fix an unknown jetty and provide logistical support to the ECN.

The address will be provided to (INSERT NAME) on Monday 05/FEB/2024.

This will be in preparation for members of the ECN to arrive in Perth on Wednesday 07/FEB/2024.

These members are then due to inspect the property and jetty to assess its capability to be used by the syndicate.

4a Operational Strategy

It is preferable that IO’s discuss their needs with the Ops Managers of the relevant unit, so they can prioritise or offer other options.

Ops Managers / OIC’s are authorised to consider urgent requests and action, but require a retrospective TRF to follow.

Please no “as discussed with Ops manager”, we don’t need war and peace, but a brief description of the activity is required.

If an operational strategy has been discussed with the relevant covert ops manager please mention this but we still require details in relation to the strategy as ops managers have lots of conversations with investigators and can’t possibly remember every conversation or strategy.

• Surveillance (SU)

Example: Utilising SU to assist investigators in establishing the movements of the target to identify associates linked to the SOCG and further identify safe houses utilised for the storage and distribution of prohibited drugs and unlawfully obtained cash.

Is PD/LBS active?

Has analysis on PD/LBS been done to identify most active times?

What do you want SU to identify?

• Safe Houses?

• Associates?

• Possession of cash counters / items of interest / packages / etc?

• Possession of large amounts of cash?

• Places frequented?

• Possible evidentiary / intelligence materials that may be identified / useful to investigators?

If available provide basic information about the network and how it is believed to operate.

• Police Technical Unit (PTU)

(Listening Devices, Optical Devices, Tracking Devices, Technical Assistance) ** If tracker install cannot be achieved in a public place or is a country location a request for SOAU

(SDA) must either be submitted on the same TRF or an additional TRF with reference that it is a

country install or public place not achievable.

Please state in the TRF if you intend to deploy a tracker in a public or private place.

If you are in an area with the capability to deploy your own tracker please indicate if the request is a self-install or if PTU are required.

Example 1: Installation of a tracking device on 1CJN447 (WA) 2006 silver Volkswagen Golf sedan (PLEASE INCLUDE FULL VEHICLE DETAILS) to identify locations of interest.

Example 2: Installation of Audio/Optical at storage facility to capture criminal activity.

• Special Operations Application Unit (SOAU)

(Telecommunication Intercepts, (TI), Surveillance Devices Act warrants (SDA) Example 1: SOAU to complete SDA warrant for any tracking/optical/audio devices.

Example 2: SOAU affidavit writers requested for telephone intercept application.

• Controlled Operations Applications (CO)

Example: Utilise the services of a Controlled Operation to assist the approved operational strategy.

• Under Cover Police Unit (UPU)

Example 1: Cell bump on arrest of suspect.

Example 2: Strategy has been discussed with UPU. Intention is to ........... provide brief description.

• Online Operations

Example: To forensically capture the suspect's Instagram account.

4b Operational Outcome

What does your use of CSS units aim to achieve? Is the operation focused on evidentiary collection and resolution assistance or is it based on gaining more information about a target or target group?

Example 1: Operational objectives

1. Identify associates of the suspect/s operating within WA who collect money on behalf of the SOC

Group;

2. Identify persons responsible for the delivery of prohibited drugs;

3. identify methodologies used by the suspect and associates to undertake the criminal activity;

4. Gather intelligence and evidence which may include transport methods, cash/drug storage

locations of the methylamphetamine believed to be in WA.

5. Identify any Further drug transactions by identified suspects and cash collects by the identified

party facilitating the role as runner/cash storage;

6. The above objectives will support future overt strategies and to enable resolution phases under a

separate operation with intelligence supported by this Operation.

7. Investigating unit to oversee recruitment opportunities in consultation with HSMU.

Example 2

To identify target's Established Criminal Network (ECN) operating within Western Australia as well as their methodologies for remitting money to NSW, concealing and distributing illicit commodities into Western Australia. This will assist with executing covert and overt investigative actions on identified persons, addresses or vehicles associated with ECN groups.

Example 3: Facilitate and capture the setup of manufacture equipment for the purpose of manufacturing methylamphetamine. Seizure of commodities and implication of further suspects to disrupt and/or dismantle established criminal network involving the suspect and his syndicate.

5 Risk Assessment

Example 1: Surveillance / no surveillance has been conducted on the target.

Example 2: All risks considered; risk mitigation has been considered as part of approved strategy.

Example 3: Suspect has a current MDL. Suspect is a high-ranking member of LWOMCG.

Suspect has 11 criminal charges including: possession of stolen or unlawfully obtained property, possess prohibited drug with intent to sell or supply. IMS warnings for OMCG associations and drug use.

6 Human Resource Requirements

Each unit to provide and manage their own resources as necessary.

Investigating Unit to provide security vehicles as requested.

8 Equipment Requirements

Each business unit will supply their own equipment.

9 Funding Requirements

* A formal Controlled Operation has been endorsed by the Commander for the proposed activity. *

For funding requests ensure the amount is recorded and a description of the strategy (Section 4a).

Funding requests are review by a Commander and you will have to justify the request. Also select correct CPF/TOF drop down at top of TRF.
